_meta.author: OpenAI ChatGPT for Peter Higgins
_meta.purpose: Capture the current scientific assessment of EITT/CoDa/HIVP with emphasis on the nuclear programme, the prime-step staircase idea, and the best next steps toward a careful stable-island prediction.

executive_assessment.summary: The science side is now coherent enough to support a disciplined predictive programme, but not yet strong enough to justify a sharp single-nuclide claim on prime-step structure alone.
current_position: EITT is strongest as an instrument for detecting constrained compositional manifolds and their lock range.
In the nuclear programme, the SEMF decomposition is currently the physically strongest compositional basis.
Sigma-squared-A is behaving like a compositional heat-capacity map, with the superheavy region becoming cooler and smoother.
F17 is best treated as an integrity / contamination channel, not as a direct predictor of stability.
The prime-step staircase is promising as a shell / packing overlay, but must remain secondary until it beats null models.
executive_assessment.bottom_line: Use EITT to predict a stable corridor first, then use shell, magic-number, and staircase/prime overlays to rank candidate nuclei inside that corridor.

solid_findings: Geometric-mean decimation is the correct compositional operator; arithmetic decimation introduces persistent bias.
The EITT slope is the key diagnostic: flat means locked, rising means losing lock.
The four-instrument architecture is now justified: EITT, velocity, delta, and trajectory efficiency / process controls remain separate.
The blind test supports instrument status: Pass 1 = 17/20, Pass 2 = 18/20 with honest resolution boundaries.
In the SEMF programme, the superheavy region A=210-295 is smooth under the chosen decomposition and shows low sigma-squared-A.
nuclear_specific: Iron peak behaves like a thermodynamic critical region.
Light nuclei are off-critical and high-variance.
Post-iron, heavy, and superheavy regions are increasingly low-variance under SEMF composition.

warnings: The valley / SEMF programme can identify broad stability structure but does not by itself resolve exact shell closures.
Shuffled valley and some noisy controls can pass, so EITT alone is not sufficient for exact island localisation.
The prime-step staircase has visual suggestiveness but is not yet a validated statistical predictor.
Error correction should not be used to force the prediction; contamination-aware process control is allowed, direct signal correction is not.
forbidden_overclaim: Do not claim that prime numbers cause nuclear stability.
Do not claim that EITT alone has already identified the exact center of the island of stability.
Do not present one isotope as a settled discovery unless it survives shell-theory and null-model checks.

broad_corridor_prediction.statement: The present EITT x SEMF evidence supports a broad superheavy stability corridor rather than a pinpoint.
why_this_is_the_right_form: External shell-based theory already brackets the island near N=184 with competing proton closures in the 114-126 range.
Internal EITT x SEMF work shows the entire superheavy region is low-variance and pass-like, which supports a broad cooled basin rather than a sharp isolated spike.
The prime-step staircase may help rank locations inside that basin, but it does not yet narrow the corridor with sufficient rigor.

single_best_internal_prediction_if_force.statement: If one operational target must be named now, use an N=184 nucleus in the low-120 Z corridor, with Z=120 or Z=122 as working candidates for ranking studies, not as final claims.
single_best_internal_prediction_if_force.confidence: moderate for corridor, low for exact Z

what_the_plot_suggests: The staircase N(Z) along the valley is not random; step sizes are structured.
Sigma-squared-A along the valley smooths downward with Z, consistent with the heat-capacity reading.
Prime-marked proton numbers appear visually near some large-step or inflection regions.
Big-step proximity to primes may be capturing shell/packing boundaries rather than primality as a causal mechanism.
prime_step_staircase_assessment.most_likely_interpretation: Prime Z is best treated as a proxy overlay for sparse arithmetic packing, shell spacing, or staircase irregularity, not as the underlying physics.
null_models_required: Randomized prime-label control with same Z support
Magic-number control (compare prime proximity versus magic-number proximity)
Distance-to-nearest-prime baseline versus random integer baseline
Local-window enrichment test for large steps near prime Z
prime_step_staircase_assessment.promotion_rule: The prime-step layer stays exploratory until it beats shell-only and random-baseline models on held-out regions.

recommended_prediction_stack.decision_rule: Only make a strong prediction where tier 1 and tier 2 agree. Use tier 3 only to rank candidates inside that pre-approved region.

immediate: Freeze the nuclear report vocabulary: corridor, shell overlay, staircase score, resolution boundary.
Standardize a sliding-window nuclear dashboard with sigma-squared-A, EITT pass rate, EITT slope, velocity, and shell markers.
Run prime-step staircase statistics against null models before using it in any headline claim.
Separate SEMF smooth structure from shell residuals explicitly rather than mixing them.
short_horizon: Build a two-layer nuclear model: layer A = SEMF compositional basin, layer B = shell / staircase residual map.
Rank candidate superheavy nuclei by combined score: low local sigma-squared-A + flat slope + shell support + staircase anomaly support.
Test whether the prime/staircase signal peaks near known shell candidates before extrapolating beyond observed masses.
hardening: Re-run the nuclear prediction pipeline under HIVP after every scoring change.
Require no degradation to EXP-01 through EXP-05 core behaviour.
Add explicit uncertainty classes: corridor-level, regional-level, single-isotope-level.

claude_instruction.exact_text: Treat the current nuclear EITT programme as strong enough to predict a superheavy stability corridor, but not yet strong enough to claim an exact island center from the prime-step staircase alone. Use SEMF + shell physics as the primary basis, EITT as the structural/thermodynamic meter, and the prime-step idea only as a secondary ranking overlay until it survives null-model tests.
